news.headline: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability
news.summary: The United Nations has welcomed reports of a new ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah on Friday while warning that civilians on the ground are still fleeing amid ongoing insecurity.

news.headline: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
news.summary: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.

news.headline: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
news.summary: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

news.headline: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
news.summary: The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday.

news.headline: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
news.summary: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

news.headline: Libya’s political process regains momentum, but window for action is narrowing, UN envoy warns
news.summary: Libya has been mired in political dysfunction since the collapse of Muammar Gaddafi’s regime in 2011, which shattered State institutions and triggered recurring struggles over legitimacy and power.&nbsp;

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
news.summary: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly

news.headline: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
news.summary: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.

news.headline: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
news.summary: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

news.headline: ‘Darkest chapter’: Record child violations in 2025, with national forces leading the way
news.summary: For the first time, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups – and 2025 set a grim new record for the total number of child victims.&nbsp;

news.headline: Worsening hunger could push millions closer to famine in 13 global hotspots
news.summary: Millions of people across some of the world’s most vulnerable regions are expected to face worsening hunger in the months ahead, as conflict, economic pressures and shrinking aid budgets exacerbate long-running crises.

news.headline: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
news.summary: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on women, peace and security
news.summary: The UN Security Council met in New York Wednesday for its annual open debate on women, peace and security, in line with a 25-year-old resolution. The Secretary-General’s most recent report on the issue shows that in 2024, women made up only seven percent of negotiators on average worldwide, and near

news.headline: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
news.summary: Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak.

news.headline: Armed Boats Attack Two Commercial Ships Off Yemen In Separate Gulf of Aden Incidents - Marine Insight
news.summary: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMitgFBVV95cUxQQVhNN1RkcW9zcUlGVVRyMHZLbm04Ynpwd2J3VVBqRnFYLWpEenlBU1UyRDVjTktIYmpRUFRlNXlYeVRvZUcyOGRwTkpqb2pCZ0x4VElWSTlRVVg2N043OHlXcHRqZXdfdzJYd0t6aGV0M1NNM3VWT2lpVlFHb0czSDQyT2daM095bVk0UFRNNzA0UGI1ZHh3cUpiVkEtQ05Kcy0ycHRocG1uVm5iczQxbkxERWpzdw?

news.headline: A turning point for Haiti? New security force takes fight to powerful gangs
news.summary: Passengers look out of the back of a brightly-coloured shared pick-up style taxi, known locally in Haiti as a “tap tap” as it makes its way along the Boulevard du 15 Octobre in the east of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

news.headline: UN officials call for urgent action in Yemen to push peace, reduce hunger
news.summary: Top UN officials called for bolstered efforts to stave off rising hunger and foster a path towards permanent peace in Yemen as they briefed the Security Council Tuesday morning.

news.headline: ‘Some question if Ebola is real’: How trust is central in fighting DRC outbreak
news.summary: In Ebola-stricken Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), winning the race against the disease requires earning the community’s trust first and foremost, humanitarians said on Tuesday.

news.summary: <a href="https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMinAFBVV95cUxNdjhtZU1OUkoxcmQ5WEx6Yk9jcUh5VkxMc0VOa3d1TTVXZGZfYzBWVGZXQ1lVemw4MTF5RHZoY3gzQ09aOXpVS3BuUm1BamthTHZLa0xsOW5kSTlvSTdfTUx5aWZJSk1HMG1pdUI0V25lZXJfZFRfT3BjQmNPTFh3aU5OZFJITjVxZ2s3SWQzZG5hS3ZkcldvOVRGXzc?oc=5" target="_blank">Armed Skif

news.headline: World News in Brief: Risky return home in Lebanon, displacement in Gaza, emergency funding for Somalia
news.summary: Despite the agreement announced on Sunday between the United States and Iran, displaced people in Lebanon continue to face difficulties in returning to their communities, the UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA) has reported.&nbsp;

news.headline: Ukraine: Latest Russian attack kills civilians, damages cultural landmark
news.summary: Several civilians were killed and dozens more were injured in the latest wave of overnight attacks in Ukraine that targeted the capital Kyiv, the city of Kharkiv and the country’s history and cultural heritage, the United Nations said on Monday.

news.headline: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
news.summary: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

news.headline: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
news.summary: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission.

news.headline: Sudan civilians trapped by fear, disappearance and detention: rights experts
news.summary: As Sudan’s conflict enters a fourth year, civilians are increasingly trapped not only by frontline violence but by fear, disappearance and detention, according to an update by the Independent International Fact-Finding Mission for the Sudan to the 62nd Session of the UN Human Rights Council in Genev

news.headline: Israel Strikes Beirut Outskirts as Fighting With Hezbollah Escalates - The New York Times

news.headline: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
news.summary: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.

news.headline: Refugee turned peacemaker: The young woman leader transforming communities in the Central African Republic
news.summary: In the heart of the Central African Republic (CAR), a nation long marked by conflict, Nina Mireille Yankinon stands as a symbol of resilience, leadership, and hope, dedicating her life to helping communities torn apart by war.&nbsp;

news.headline: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
news.summary: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.

news.headline: Security Council weighs future of UN war crimes mechanism as closure nears
news.summary: The Security Council on Friday weighed the future of the UN mechanism responsible for completing the remaining work of the international tribunals for Rwanda and the former Yugoslavia, as members debated how to preserve its legacy while bringing its mandate to an orderly close.

news.headline: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
news.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emerge

news.headline: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
news.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

news.headline: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
news.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

news.headline: DR Congo: Ebola spreads as agencies brace for child victims
news.summary: The deadly Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is continuing to spread with a spike in child infections an increasingly likely scenario in the days ahead, UN agencies said on Friday.

news.headline: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
news.summary: Gender equality agency&nbsp;UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.&nbsp;&nbsp;

news.headline: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
news.summary: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.

news.headline: Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief
news.summary: Nearly four months after the latest Middle East crisis erupted and despite a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, tensions continue to reverberate across the region and beyond. news.headline: Fresh strikes on Tyre kill eight, as UN puts Lebanon destruction bill at $365 million, and rising
news.summary: Lebanon has suffered more than $365 million in damage to buildings across Beirut and Mount Lebanon since the latest escalation, according to a new UN-led assessment released on Tuesday, as fresh strikes in Tyre underscored the fragility of a ceasefire that has failed to stop the fighting.

news.headline: Palestinians face systematic abuse by Israeli settlers and Hamas alike: Independent investigators
news.summary: Palestinian civilians are trapped between escalating settler violence in the occupied West Bank and fear-based Hamas rule in the Gaza Strip, investigators appointed by the UN Human Rights Council said on Tuesday in a new report.

news.headline: Sudan war: Drone attacks damage key aid routes
news.summary: Escalating attacks on bridges, roads and other civilian infrastructure in Sudan are disrupting humanitarian access and putting civilians at further risk, the United Nations said on Tuesday.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Central Africa in the spotlight, including outlawed Lord's Resistance Army
news.summary: The Security Council meets this morning to discuss the situation in Central Africa, including efforts to combat the so-called Lord’s Resistance Army (LRA), one of the region’s longest-running security threats, responsible for over 100,000 deaths, the UN estimates. Ambassadors have been briefed by th

news.headline: Building trust and lab testing at the heart of DRC Ebola response: WHO
news.summary: In Ebola-stricken eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) a massive push for early testing and contact tracing is helping to&nbsp;contain the virus, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.

news.headline: ‘Rare, untreatable strain’: Ebola toll mounts in eastern DR Congo
news.summary: The top UN aid official in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is in Ituri province – the epicentre of the country's Ebola outbreak – for a three-day assessment visit, as the confirmed case count reaches 515 across three eastern provinces.
